title: FixtureManifest
description: A per-adapter x per-mode conformance fixture. The (future) standalone conformance runner replays `command` against a stubbed CLI, feeds `input`, and asserts the adapter's AdapterResult matches `expect`. This schema is the contract that runner consumes; the runner itself is a follow-up issue and is out of scope here.

input.description: The invoke() inputs (prompt is referenced by byte-length rather than inlined so the manifest stays small and the prompt body can be a separate fixture file).

promptBytes.description: Length in bytes of the prompt fed on stdin (INV-34).

model.description: The model id passed to invoke() (the resolved per-agent review/dev model).

env.description: Environment variables the adapter reads (e.g. AGENT_REVIEW_EXTRA_ARGS_CODEX, CODEX_REVIEW_NO_WORKTREE_RC). Values are recorded verbatim.

command.description: The CLI invocation the stub records: the exact argv, the stdin hash, and the canned process result.

argv.description: The full argv the adapter assembled (e.g. ["codex", "review", "<prompt>"] for codex review, INV-62).

stdinSha256.description: SHA-256 (hex) of the bytes the adapter wrote to the CLI's stdin. Pins the INV-34 stdin-fed-prompt contract.

files.description: Sidecar / log / artifact files the stub stages or the adapter is expected to write (e.g. the codex thread-id sidecar, the agy --log-file, the E2E AC-coverage sidecar). Keyed by logical name; value is the file path (relative to the fixture root) and an optional expected-content hash.

expect.description: The AdapterResult axes the adapter MUST produce for this fixture. The runner asserts these against the real adapter's output.

retryable.description: Whether the dispatcher MAY re-dispatch this adapter for the same work (true for transient; false for quota/auth/config until the operator acts).
